name: DealCloud
one_liner: Intapp DealCloud is a deal, pipeline, and relationship management (CRM) platform for capital-markets firms such as private equity, investment banking, and law firms.
primary_auth: OAuth2 client-credentials flow: a user-generated API key plus client_id are exchanged at {site}/api/rest/v1/oauth/token for a scoped bearer token (expires every 900 seconds).
access_notes: API keys are self-serve from the user Profile page, but only inside a live DealCloud site: an admin must enable the API capability for the user's group, and DealCloud itself is enterprise software sold via request-a-demo (roughly $85k+/yr, no free trial or public sandbox).
api_notes: Well-documented REST API covering schema/metadata, data rows and cells (CRUD, query, merge, history, files), user management, publications (event pub/sub), site backups, relationship intelligence, and templated reports - roughly 45-60 operations, with per-site Swagger/OpenAPI plus official Python and C# SDKs.
mcp_notes: The official developer docs announce a vendor-shipped 'DealCloud MCP Server' in client preview starting July 2026; detailed docs (tools, auth) are not yet published. No community MCP servers found in searches.
main_blocker: No public API access without an existing Intapp DealCloud enterprise contract - no trial, sandbox, or self-serve signup path to credentials.

evidence.claim: DealCloud API uses OAuth2 client-credentials flow exchanging client_id + API key for a bearer token that expires after 900 seconds
API keys are enabled self-serve from the user Profile page after an admin grants the user group the API site-area capability
Developer docs cover REST APIs for schema, data, users, publications, backups, and relationship intelligence, plus Python/C# SDKs and an MCP Server client preview starting July 2026
DealCloud Publications are a polling-based pub/sub event system (default-data, default-schema, default-users topics), not outbound push webhooks
DealCloud is enterprise software with no public pricing or free trial; access is via a schedule-a-demo sales process

open_questions: Exact endpoint count and API versioning scheme not confirmed (token endpoint is v1; other routes may differ). MCP server details (transport, auth, exposed tools) unpublished while in client preview. Could not confirm whether Intapp offers any partner/developer sandbox outside customer contracts.
